description: Stable keys are required to preserve component and form identity across list updates.
body: Use when list edits, reordering, transitions, or form rows behave incorrectly. Do not use array index as key when item identity can change, especially for editable rows, transitions, and child components with local state. Use stable domain IDs and verify reorder/add/remove interactions in rendered UI. Risk boundary: state leakage between rows can corrupt user input or persisted edits. Use when Vue Frontend Engineering work touches visible UI behavior, browser console output, DOM state, assets, viewport behavior, and interaction evidence. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include browser smoke, component tests, viewport screenshots, console/network checks, and accessibility assertions. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves visual regressions, inaccessible interaction, hydration mismatch, and browser-only failures, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
